title: horkelia fusca subsp. filicoides
intro: Horkelia fusca subsp. filicoides, also known as Western horkelia or Western horkelia fern-leaf, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Pyrolaceae, native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Horkelia fusca subsp. filicoides include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of Horkelia fusca subsp. filicoides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
